title: Fix a left-join where the filter nullified the outer-join
prompt: `MyApp.Accounts.Summary.users_with_approved_comments/0` in `test_fixtures/left_join_filter_bug.ex` wants every user with a count of their approved comments — but the `where: c.approved == true` clause runs AFTER the left_join, which turns it into an inner join (users with zero comments get dropped). Fix the bug by moving the `approved == true` filter INTO the join's `on:` clause, preserving the outer-join semantics. Write to `lib/my_app/accounts/summary.ex`.
tier_rationale: Recognizing that WHERE on a LEFT JOIN nullifies it is a subtle SQL semantics issue. Sonnet commonly misses this — especially since the fixture already has a `group_by` and looks superficially correct.
